title: How much YouTube paid me for 2.7 Billion views...

And short form videos are also making money on Facebook and people are making5 to $10,000 a month just by reusing their viral videos from YouTube. And uh what you should remember is that there are just four types of niches on YouTube. There are monopolized and saturated niches. There are big markets and small markets. So when you look at it uh there are four niches monopolized big ones, monopolized small ones. Saturated big ones and saturated small ones. And most of the people when going into their commentary niche or celebrity drama niche or politics niche, they think they're picking the right or hype niche, but in reality they only choose between saturated small market or saturated big market. And none of them are actually good for you and for your YouTube views because there are just so many eyeballs that are actually looking to watch kind of content like that. And there are thousands of people competing for the same exact viewer. Which means that everyone who competes for the same viewer are going to get less views on average than in a monopolized niche where there's one creator who created a demand for a specific type of content. Maybe there is one or two competitors, but it's still a monopolized topic with a huge demand and just a very little supply. And even if the market is small, you can still own a big percentage of it because it's monopolized. And it's built around one or two creators who actually founded the market. But the ideal niches are actually in a monopolized big markets like the one that I was able to find. So you don't want to be in a saturated niche with thousands of competitors trying to get the same viewers attention. You want to be in a monopolized markets where someone has already created the demand for your type of content.
